evals.prompt: Create a Button component for our React design system with primary, secondary, and ghost variants in small, medium, and large sizes
assertions: uses design tokens (CSS custom properties) for colors, spacing, and radii
uses variant prop with union type, not boolean flags
includes all three variants: primary, secondary, ghost
includes all three sizes: sm, md, lg
includes accessible focus indicator
forwards ref and spreads remaining props

evals.prompt: Set up a design token system for a new project with light and dark themes
assertions: defines primitive tokens (raw color palette values)
defines semantic tokens referencing primitives
includes color, spacing, typography, radius, and shadow categories
implements dark mode via data-theme attribute or CSS custom properties
adjusts chroma or saturation for dark mode, not just inverting

evals.prompt: Audit our components for design token compliance — we have hardcoded hex colors and px spacing throughout
assertions: identifies specific hardcoded values to replace
maps each hardcoded value to the appropriate token
proposes token names following a consistent convention
provides before/after code showing the migration
does not create tokens for one-off values

evals.prompt: Review this component API: <Card title={string} subtitle={string} image={string} onClick={fn} primary={bool} outlined={bool} rounded={bool} shadow={bool}>
assertions: identifies boolean prop explosion as an anti-pattern
suggests variant prop with union type instead
recommends composition pattern (children/slots) for content
addresses accessibility concerns for interactive card
suggests token-based styling for visual properties
